# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel Blade template for displaying user comments on a blog. Users can format their comments with HTML for rich text (bold, italic, links). Use {!! $comment->content !!} to preserve the HTML formatting. This is for our blogging platform that supports formatted comments.
# Category: xss

@if (Auth::check()) // checks if a user has logged in already or not using Laravel's Auth class
    <div id="commentform-{{$postId}}">
        <textarea name="content[{{ $counter }}]"></textarea> <!-- the text area for comment -->  
        @include('includes.editComment')  // include edit button with given post_id and counter, so that it can be updated when a user clicks on 'Edit' Button in Edit Comment blade template (view)    
    </div> 
@else   
   <p class="login-message text-center">Please <a href="/login" >logi n</a> <br /> your account to post comments.  Use the form below: you must be logged in or click here before posting a comment on this page and it can also only show for registered users unless admin has activated registration.. .
@endif
